task_completion.description: Verify the OpenBCI GUI is open and visible on the screen.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main OpenBCI GUI application window is present.

task_completion.description: Verify the Time Series widget is displayed within the OpenBCI GUI.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A widget labeled 'Time Series' showing EEG waveforms is visible in the interface.

task_completion.description: Verify that other EEG channels are active and displaying signals.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Waveforms for channels other than Channel 3 (e.g., 1, 2, 4-8) show active, fluctuating signals typical of a Synthetic EEG session.

task_completion.description: Locate the channel toggle button for Channel 3.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A button labeled '3' is visible on the left edge of the Time Series widget, corresponding to the third channel.

task_completion.description: Verify the Channel 3 toggle button has been clicked and disabled.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The channel toggle button labeled '3' displays an 'X' over it or next to it, indicating it is turned off.

task_completion.description: Verify the waveform for Channel 3 is disabled.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The graph/waveform corresponding to Channel 3 is a completely flat line, unlike the active fluctuating signals of the other channels.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted directly with the graphical user interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent's actions (like mouse clicks) occur within the OpenBCI GUI window. There should be no terminal commands or scripts used to bypass the UI interaction.

integrity.description: Verify the interaction happened in a live application session.
integrity.visual_evidence: The other EEG channels should show continuous, active waveforms, indicating a live Synthetic EEG session rather than a static image or text editor.

integrity.description: Verify the genuine OpenBCI GUI application is being used.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface matches the standard OpenBCI GUI layout, including proper branding, widgets, and controls, rather than a mock-up or alternative software.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task environment runs OpenBCI GUI v5.2.2. To verify EEG Channel 3 is disabled, look at the left edge of the Time Series widget. The channel toggle button labeled '3' must show an 'X', and the corresponding waveform for Channel 3 must be a flat line.
